appendix.title: Instance Configuration Files
appendix.para: This section describe each of the configuration parameter used by SilkPage to publish an instance of your website. This information is most useful when publishing a particular instance of a Website.

section.para: /build.properties contains main configuration of an instance website.

section.title: XSL and CSS Customization Parameters
section.para: This section covers the customization and configuration parameters at the XSL and CSS layers. SilkPage uses SilkPage XSL Themes to process and generate your website. You may either use the default XSL themes or plug in yours. Likewise, SilkPage CSS Themes are used in order to present your website. Again, you may either use default SilkPage CSS Themes or define and use yours.

refnamediv.refpurpose: XSL configuration parameters

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the main XSL configuration parameters used when generating your website. Refer to to learn about the configuration parameters of this file.

refnamediv.refpurpose:
Specifies the CSS Theme used to generate your website. SilkPage comes with some pre-defined CSS Themes that you can select. However, you can write your own CSS Theme and install it under
SILKPAGE_HOME/src/css . Note that SILKPAGE_HOME is the install directory of the SilkPage software. Currently, SilkPage includes the following CSS themes.

Specifies the XSL Theme used to generate your website. SilkPage comes with some pre-defined XSL Themes that you can select. However, you can write your own XSL Theme and install it under SILKPAGE_HOME/src/xsl. Note that SILKPAGE_HOME is the install directory of the SilkPage software. Currently, SilkPage includes the following XSL themes.

section.title: DocBook Parameters
section.para: This section covers configuration parameters related to DocBook resources. That is the installation directories of the required DocBook resources.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Docbook XML installation directory.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the installation directory of the DocBook XML. This should be a fully qualified path.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Docbook XSL Stylesheets installation directory.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the installation directory of the DocBook XSL Stylesheets. This should be a fully qualified path.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Docbook Website DTD and Stylesheets installation directory.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the installation directory of the DocBook Website DTD and Stylesheets. This should be a fully qualified path.

refnamediv.refpurpose: An internal database for resolving links.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the fully qualified name to an auto-generated database file used by SilkPage for resolving links. This should be a fully qualified path. On Windows platorms, the path MUST start with a '/'.

refnamediv.refpurpose: The global glossary file for resolving terms and acronyms.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the fully qualified name to the site glossary file. This file may contain a collection of terms and brief descriptions or definitions of those terms. Once you refer to a term in your site content, SilkPage transforms it to an HTML acronym.

section.title: FTP Parameters
section.para: This section covers the configuration parameters used by the FTP task. This task is used when running SilkPage with the publish target. That is when publishing your Website online.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifying ftp server address

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The address of the remote ftp server where you Website is located.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifying FTP Port

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The port number of the remote ftp server. Defaults to port 21.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifying where your website will be located the ftp server

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Remote directory on the ftp server. That is your home directory on the Web server.

refnamediv.refpurpose: The login id to use on the ftp server.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the login id to use on the ftp server.

refnamediv.refpurpose: The login password to use on the ftp server.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the login password to use on the ftp server.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Transfers only new or changed files if set to "yes". Defaults to "no".

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: If specified transfers only new or changed files if set to "yes". Defaults to "no".

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies passive-mode ("yes") transfers. Defaults to "no".

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies passive-mode ("yes") transfers. Defaults to "no".

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies binary-mode ("yes") or text-mode ("no") transfers. Defaults to "yes".

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies binary-mode ("yes") or text-mode ("no") transfers. Defaults to "yes".

refnamediv.refpurpose: The local directory where XHTML files are located. By default, it's set to ${install.local.dir}. It's recommended not to change the value of this parameter.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The local directory where XHTML files are located. By default, it's set to ${install.local.dir}. It's recommended not to change the value of this parameter.

section.title: General Parameters
section.para: This section covers the general configuration parameters that are used when running SilkPage with variuos targets.

refnamediv.refpurpose:
The encoding used by various tasks. Defaults to UTF-8. Please refer to
Character Sets and Encoding to learn more about avaiable encoding.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The encoding used by various Ant tasks.

refnamediv.refpurpose: The ISO language code used to locate the XML source files.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para:
The ISO language code used to locate the XML source files. Defaults to en. Please refer to
Code for the representation of names of languages to learn more about avaiable ISO 639 language codes.

refnamediv.refpurpose: The temporary directory used for performing the build tasks.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The temporary directory used for performing the build tasks. Defaults to the default temp. directory of your local host.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Your Website URL.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Your Website URL. Defaults to the site.id that you provide when running SilkPage with scratch target.

section.para: When running SilkPage with the scratch target, it generates (among other things) a default CSS stylesheet for your Website. The name of the CSS styleshhet is based on the siteid argument that you provide when running SilkPage with the scratch target. This styleshhet contains one import declaration that is an instruction to import some other default style sheets from the SilkPage CSS Themes . You may modify the import declaration and instruct it to import your own CSS stylesheets. You can even add your CSS stylesheets globally by defining your own CSS Theme and instruct SilkPage to use your CSS themes instead of its default. Refer to to learn how you can customize SilkPage by adding your CSS themes.
/src/xsl/config.xsl is the main XSL configuration parameter file used by SilkPage when processing your XML sources.

section.title: Search Field Parameters
section.para: SilkPage can generate a Google based seearch field for your Website. This section covers this search field and its associated parameters.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Enables/Disables the generation of the search field for your Website. Defaults to 1, that is enabled.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the label text that is displayed right before the search field.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the label text that is displayed right before the search field. Defaults to an empty label.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the label text that is displayed as the search submit button label.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the label text that is displayed as the search submit button label. Defaults to Search label.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Defines an XSL template containing your Website specific information that is used by Google when searhing your Website.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Defines an XSL template containing your Website specific information that is used by Google when searhing your Website. By default, the URL of your Website specified in is used in this template.

section.title: Footer Link Items Parameters
section.para: If instructed, SilkPage generate a footer item links at the footer of each generated Webpage in XHTML format. This section covers the configuration parameters for these link items.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the text for the Site Info link item at the footer of each generated page.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the text for the Site Info link item at the footer of each generated page. Defaults to "Site Info" label.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the text for the Sitemap link item at the footer of each generated page.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the text for the Sitemap link item at the footer of each generated page. Defaults to "Sitemap" label.

section.title: XML Feeds Link Items Parameters
section.para: If instructed, SilkPage generates link items for XML feeds such as RSS and RDF for each generated Webpage in XHTML format. This section covers the configuration parameters for these link items.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the text for the XML feeds source link item. Defaults to "RDF" label.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the text for the XML feeds source link item. Defaults to "RDF" label.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the text for the XML feeds source link item.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the text for the XML feeds source link item. Defaults to "RSS" label.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Specifies the text for the XML source link item.

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: Specifies the text for the XML source link item. Defaults to "XML" label.
